channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Steve Jobs a perfect choice for California’s $1 innovation coin
item.description: An iconic image of the late co-founder of Apple will be featured on the coin, which is expected to be released next year.
item.dc:creator: Sal Pizarro, The Mercury News

item.title: DOGE efforts to access private data spark sharp pushback
item.description: Elon Musk and the Department of Government Efficiency’s (DOGE) efforts to tap into databases across the government is facing broad pushback from a variety of groups sounding the alarm over the privacy and security risks.
item.dc:creator: Rebecca Beitsch, The Hill

item.title: Google caught in crossfire over Gulf of America name change
item.description: Google has found itself back in the crosshairs of a geopolitical battle over President Trump’s renaming of the Gulf of Mexico as the Gulf of America, raising fresh questions over the role technology companies can play in reinforcing political narratives.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: The iPhone 16E is here. Here are 5 ways it’s different from the iPhone SE
item.description: Apple has introduced its first new product of 2025: the iPhone 16E. The new iPhone replaces the iPhone SE from Apple’s lineup—the company’s entry-level, budget iPhone.
item.dc:creator: Michael Grothaus, Fast Company

item.title: Crackdown on power-guzzling data centers may soon come online in California
item.description: A spate of new bills in California and other states would protect consumers from electricity rate hikes that subsidize data centers.
item.dc:creator: CalMatters, The Mercury News

item.title: Oracle extends support for on-prem Oracle Database 19c
item.description: As the status of an on-premises version of Oracle Database 23ai remains in limbo, the company has tossed a bone to its on-prem Oracle Database 19c customers in the form of an extension of Premier Support to Dec. 31, 2029, and Extended Support to Dec. 31, 2032.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Apple unveils a souped-up and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone
item.description: Apple has released a sleeker and more expensive version of its lowest priced iPhone in an attempt to widen the audience for a bundle of artificial intelligence technology that the company has been hoping will revive demand for its most profitable product lineup.
item.dc:creator: Michael Liedtke, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Want to transform networking? Empower the missing users
item.description: As 2025 kicked off, I wrote a column about the network vendor landscape – specifically, which networking players will step up and put their efforts into finding new applications with solid business benefits that could enable a network transformation.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Mistral turns focus toward regional LLMs with Saba release
item.description: French AI startup Mistral is turning its focus toward providing large language models (LLMs) that understand regional languages and their parlance as a result of rising demand among its enterprise customers. “Making AI ubiquitous requires addressing every culture and language.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Key strategies for MLops success in 2025
item.description: Integrating and managing artificial intelligence and machine learning effectively within business operations has become a top priority for businesses looking to stay competitive in an ever evolving landscape.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: A top Chinese official tours Thai-Myanmar border to highlight crackdown on scam centers
item.description: BANGKOK (AP) — Efforts to shut down online scam centers in Myanmar appeared to gain momentum on Monday as a top Chinese security official visited both sides of the Thai-Myanmar border ahead of expected large-scale repatriations of workers in the illicit industry.
item.dc:creator: JINTAMAS SAKSORNCHAI, Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: What changes to the CHIPS act could mean for AI growth, consumers
item.description: Since taking office, Trump has said he would place tariffs on foreign production of computer chips and semiconductors in order to return chip manufacturing to the U.S.
item.dc:creator: SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, NewsNation
